docs.title: SSCC 2026 – Quantum Chemistry Hands‑on
docs.text:
This page provides a short orientation and step‑by‑step overview for the SSCC 2026 Quantum Chemistry hands‑on sessions.

All practical work is carried out using Jupyter notebooks in CSC's computing environment.

MFA required before you start

The Mahti web interface requires multi-factor authentication (MFA). If you haven't activated it yet, do so now — it takes a few minutes: Haka MFA · CSC MFA

docs.title: 1. Access the computing environment
docs.text:
All hands‑on sessions run via CSC Jupyter for Courses using the Mahti web interface: https://www.mahti.csc.fi

→ See: Environment

docs.title: 2. Start with the core session
docs.text:
All participants begin with the core quantum chemistry session, which introduces the basic ORCA workflows used throughout the hands‑on.

This part is common to everyone and provides the foundation for all other sessions.

docs.title: 3. Choose additional sessions
docs.text:
After completing the core session, choose optional sessions according to your interests:

  • Multireference electronic structure methods
  • Electronic structure and molecular simulation with CP2K

→ See: Workflow & module reference

docs.title: 4. Use supporting tools if needed
docs.text: Avogadro2 is available via Mahti Open OnDemand as an optional graphical tool for building molecular structures and analysing ORCA output.

docs.title: Where to find live information
docs.text:
Course schedule, announcements, and Q&A are on the SSCC 2026 HedgeDoc:

👉 https://siili.rahtiapp.fi/sscc2026

The notebooks contain detailed step‑by‑step instructions for each session.

docs.title: Molecular structure building and analysis with Avogadro2
docs.text:
Avogadro2 is a graphical molecular editor and analysis tool available via the Mahti Open OnDemand (OOD) service.

Use it to:

  • Build molecular structures and export coordinates for use in ORCA or CP2K
  • Generate ORCA input files via the built-in input generator
  • Analyse ORCA output — visualise molecular orbitals, animate vibrational modes, and plot IR spectra by opening the .out file directly

docs.title: Accessing Avogadro2 on Mahti
docs.text:
1. Log in to the Mahti web interface: https://www.mahti.csc.fi
  2. Select Desktop and launch a session
  3. Once the desktop has loaded, open a terminal

  4. Load the Avogadro2 module: 

        module use /projappl/project_2017403/modules
    module load avogadro2

  5. Launch Avogadro2: 

        avogadro2

docs.title: Structure building
docs.text:
1. Build or import a structure — use the draw tool or File → Open to load an existing file (.xyz, .mol2, etc.)
  2. Clean up the geometry — use Extensions → Open Babel → Optimize Geometry for a quick force-field pre-optimisation
  3. Export coordinates — File → Save As → choose .xyz format
  4. Use the exported file as the coordinate input in your ORCA or CP2K notebook

docs.title: Analysing ORCA output
docs.text:
1. Open the ORCA output file directly: File → Open → select .out file
  2. Molecular orbitals — select the orbital of interest in the Molecular Orbitals panel and click Render (requires !PRINTMOS !PRINTBASIS or the orbital printout option in the input generator)
  3. Vibrational modes — Analysis → Vibrational Modes to animate modes and plot the IR spectrum (requires a frequency calculation)

docs.title: Tips
docs.text:
• Avogadro2 runs inside the Desktop session — keep that session alive while using it
  • Copy-paste between your local machine and the desktop works via the noVNC clipboard: open the Clipboard menu from the tab on the left edge of the desktop window, paste your text there, then paste normally inside the desktop
  • A force-field pre-optimisation in Avogadro2 before running ORCA saves time by providing a reasonable starting geometry

docs.title: Working environment
docs.text:
The SSCC 2026 Quantum Chemistry hands‑on uses CSC Jupyter for Courses, accessed via the Mahti web interface.

All hands‑on work is carried out in this environment. No local installation or software setup is required.

The notebooks rely on:

  • CSC‑provided installations of ORCA and CP2K
  • A custom Python environment prepared for the course

docs.title: Access
docs.text:
Log in to the Mahti web interface using your CSC or Haka account:

👉 https://www.mahti.csc.fi

MFA required

The Mahti web interface requires multi-factor authentication (MFA). Ensure MFA is active before the hands-on session.

  • Activate MFA for Haka accounts
  • Activate MFA for CSC accounts

docs.title: Starting a notebook session
docs.text:
1. Go to https://www.mahti.csc.fi
  2. Select Jupyter for Courses

  3. Fill in the form:

    Field Value Project project_2017403 Course module see table below Reservation see table below Partition small (or as indicated)

  4. Click Launch and wait for the session to start (typically under a minute)

  5. Once the Jupyter environment has opened, navigate to the notebook indicated during the session

docs.title: Which module do I pick?
docs.text:
Session Course module Reservation Core QC workflows (Thu) sscc-2026-qc sscc_thu_small Multireference methods (Thu, optional) sscc-2026-qc-mr sscc_thu_small CP2K (Thu, optional) sscc-2026-cp2k sscc_thu_small PES exploration (Fri) sscc-2026-qc-pes sscc_fri_small

Partition is small for all sessions except CP2K batch jobs (medium) — see Workflow for details.

docs.title: During the session
docs.text:
• The notebooks are largely self‑contained — instructions are in the cells
  • Do not hesitate to ask for help if something is unclear

All required software is preconfigured in the environment.

docs.title: References and acknowledgements
docs.text: The SSCC 2026 Quantum Chemistry hands-on sessions make use of the following open-source software packages. We gratefully acknowledge the work of their developers.

docs.title: ORCA
docs.text:
Neese, F.; Wennmohs, F.; Becker, U.; Riplinger, C. The ORCA quantum chemistry program package. J. Chem. Phys. 2020, 152, 224108. https://doi.org/10.1063/5.0004608

👉 ORCA forum · ORCA 6 tutorials

Note

ORCA prints a list of recommended method-specific citations at the end of every output file. Please cite these in addition to the generic reference above when publishing results.

docs.title: OPI — ORCA Python Interface
docs.text:
Neugebauer, H. et al. ORCA meets Python — the ORCA Python Interface OPI. J. Chem. Theory Comput. 2025. https://doi.org/10.1021/acs.jctc.5c02141

👉 OPI GitHub · OPI documentation

docs.title: CP2K
docs.text:
Kühne, T. D. et al. CP2K: An electronic structure and molecular dynamics software package — Quickstep: Efficient and accurate electronic structure calculations. J. Chem. Phys. 2020, 152, 194103. https://doi.org/10.1063/5.0007045

👉 CP2K website · CP2K manual

docs.title: ASE — Atomic Simulation Environment
docs.text:
Larsen, A. H. et al. The atomic simulation environment — a Python library for working with atoms. J. Phys.: Condens. Matter 2017, 29, 273002. https://doi.org/10.1088/1361-648X/aa680e

👉 ASE website

docs.title: Avogadro2
docs.text:
Hanwell, M. D.; Curtis, D. E.; Lonie, D. C.; Vandermeersch, T.; Zurek, E.; Hutchison, G. R. Avogadro: An advanced semantic chemical editor, visualization, and analysis platform. J. Cheminformatics 2012, 4, 17. https://doi.org/10.1186/1758-2946-4-17

Note

This paper describes Avogadro 1.x. No separate publication for Avogadro2 exists yet; the 2012 paper remains the recommended citation.

👉 Avogadro2 website · Avogadro2 GitHub

docs.title: Thursday 9.4. — Quantum Chemistry hands‑on
docs.text:
All participants start with the core session. After that, choose one or more optional sessions based on your interests.

Session Course module Reservation Core QC workflows (start here) sscc-2026-qc sscc_thu_small Multireference methods (optional) sscc-2026-qc-mr sscc_thu_small CP2K (optional) sscc-2026-cp2k sscc_thu_small

docs.title: Friday 10.4. — Potential Energy Surface exploration
docs.text:
Independent of the Thursday sessions.

Session Course module Reservation PES exploration sscc-2026-qc-pes sscc_fri_small

Partition is small for all sessions except CP2K batch jobs (medium).

docs.title: Where to find instructions
docs.text: Detailed instructions are in the notebooks themselves. For the course schedule and Q&A, see the SSCC 2026 HedgeDoc.
